---
name: 'Documentation Architecture'
description: 'Cross-cutting rules shared by every SIMULATOR-CORE documentation agent: fixed section order, equation/notation rules, intro/developer-docs/support requirements, and the build-validation command.'
applyTo: 'doc/**/*.rst'
---

These rules are shared across all SIMULATOR-CORE documentation agents. Specialist agents
link to this file instead of repeating these rules; each specialist's own body still
defines its page-specific section order, content requirements, and source-priority file
list.

Fixed top-level section order
------------------------------
Use this order for ``doc/index.rst``, major landing pages, top-level toctrees, and section
navigation decisions, unless the user explicitly requests a different structure:

1. Introduction
2. Solver
3. Network
4. Physics
5. Control
6. Developer Documentation
7. Support

Single toctree path rule
-------------------------
Every page must be reachable through exactly one ``.. toctree::`` path. This applies
repo-wide, not just to the generated API tree: conceptual section landing pages
(Intro/Solver/Network/Physics/Control) must not toctree directly into a page that is
already toctreed from the curated ``doc/reference/`` tier or the generated API tree.
Cross-link to such a page with ``:doc:``/``:ref:`` prose instead — see
``doc/controller/controller.rst``'s "Implementation reference" section for the correct
pattern (a prose link to ``:doc:`../reference/controller_reference`` with no toctree entry
duplicating it). Linking the same page from two toctrees produces duplicate, confusing
navigation entries for the same content even though Sphinx does not warn about it.

Equation depth policy
----------------------
For physics and solver-adjacent explanations:
- Use concise engineering equations only.
- Include explicit governing relations for primary physical behaviors.
- Define symbols compactly and explain practical consequences.
- Avoid full derivations, optimization notation, and implementation-heavy formalism.

Mathematical notation rules
----------------------------
When writing equations:
- do not use ``\cdot``,
- do not use ``x`` as a multiplication symbol,
- do not use optimization-style indexed notation.

Use instead:
- implicit multiplication, for example ``m c_p DeltaT``,
- parentheses, for example ``Q = m (h_out - h_in)``,
- simple fractions/division where clearer.

Introduction requirements
--------------------------
For the introductory page and top-level overview:
- Include a short subsection that explains what OMOTES.SIMULATOR_CORE does.
- Include a short subsection that explains why it is used in practice.
- Keep this explanation accessible for both end users and developers.

Developer documentation requirements
-------------------------------------
- Keep a dedicated ``Developer Documentation`` section in the main toctree.
- API reference content under ``doc/developer/api/`` is fully generated by the existing
  ``run_apidoc()`` hook in ``doc/conf.py`` (``sphinx.ext.apidoc`` with ``--force
  --module-first --separate --no-toc`` against ``src/omotes_simulator_core``, post-processed
  by ``normalize_apidoc_titles()``). ``--no-toc`` deliberately skips generating the separate
  ``modules.rst`` wrapper file: with a single top-level package, that file would only be an
  empty toctree pointing at the root package page, so the root package page
  (``doc/developer/api/omotes_simulator_core.rst``, titled "API Root Index") is the
  generated tree's one entry point. Do not hand-edit generated pages under
  ``doc/developer/api/`` and do not reimplement or bypass this mechanism.
- The single toctree path rule above applies to the generated tree too: apidoc's root
  package page already nests every subpackage via its own ``.. toctree::``; do not also
  list the root package or its subpackages a second time elsewhere.
- Maintain the curated landing/reference tier by hand: ``doc/developer/developer_main.rst``,
  ``doc/developer/developer_navigation.rst``, and ``doc/reference/``
  (``architecture_reference.rst``, ``asset_reference.rst``, ``asset_uncovered_entities.rst``,
  ``controller_reference.rst``, ``controller_uncovered_entities.rst``,
  ``solver_reference.rst``, ``solver_uncovered_entities.rst``).
- Keep navigation flat and shallow: ``doc/developer/developer_main.rst``'s own toctree lists
  ``developer_navigation``, the four curated reference cluster pages
  (``architecture_reference.rst``, ``asset_reference.rst``, ``solver_reference.rst``,
  ``controller_reference.rst``), and the generated tree's root index
  (``api/omotes_simulator_core``) directly as siblings — all reachable in exactly 1 hop from
  ``developer_main.rst`` (2 hops from ``doc/index.rst``). Do not introduce an intermediate
  landing page between ``developer_main.rst`` and any of these — curated reference is the
  primary, prominent path; the generated tree's root index is a secondary fallback for
  exhaustive lookup, not a co-equal browsing structure.
- Keep generated reference pages separate from end-user conceptual pages.

Support page requirements
--------------------------
- Keep a dedicated ``Support`` page (``doc/support/support.rst``) in the main toctree.
- Provide clear instructions for requesting support.
- Ground support paths in what actually exists:
  - The `Project-OMOTES/user_feedback <https://github.com/Project-OMOTES/user_feedback>`_
    repository is the end-user support front door for the Design toolkit (of which
    simulator-core is a backend component): Discussions, "Q&A" category, for usage
    questions; Issues, using the ``bug_report.yml``/``feature_request.yml`` templates, for
    bug reports and feature requests.
  - ``CONTRIBUTING.md`` (branch/PR/review workflow, lint/type-check, test coverage
    guideline) and this repository's own GitHub issue tracker for contributor/development
    questions and code-level bugs in simulator-core itself.
  - ``README.md`` for package/usage overview.
- No ``.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`` directory exists in *this* repository (simulator-core) —
  do not invent templates here. Templates do exist in the external ``user_feedback``
  repository (``bug_report.yml``, ``feature_request.yml``, ``question.yml``) and may be
  referenced by name/purpose since they are real, confirmed artifacts — do not fabricate
  others.
- Keep the page concise and actionable.

Style rules
-----------
- Use neutral, technical language.
- Keep sections concise, actionable, and cross-linked.
- Prefer short paragraphs and clear headings.
- Avoid unnecessary bullets except for assumptions, limitations, and checklists.
- Output valid reStructuredText when writing ``.rst`` files.

Output format requirements
---------------------------
- Output only the final ``.rst`` page content.
- Use valid reStructuredText syntax.
- Use standard section headings and underlines.
- Do not use Markdown.
- Do not include meta-commentary, TODOs, or explanation of writing choices.

Build-validation command
-------------------------
If command execution is available, run ``doc/make.bat html`` or ``doc/run_spinx.bat``
(the repository's actual build script filename) from the repository root to check for
documentation build warnings or errors.
